feature.name: Support rect() and xywh() in shape-outside
feature.creator: cgqaq@chromium.org
feature.summary: Adds support for the rect() and xywh() basic shape functions in the CSS shape-outside property. These functions allow developers to define float exclusion shapes using rectangle coordinates, aligning Chrome with Firefox and Safari which already support this feature.

intake.finding: The ChromeStatus record (created 2026-04-02 by cgqaq@chromium.org) correctly identifies a concrete gap: Chrome's shape-outside property did not support the rect() and xywh() basic shape functions, while Firefox and Safari already did. The feature is categorized as CSS, assigned to Blink>CSS, and has 39 stars. The summary clearly states the interoperability gap and the alignment goal. The intake correctly classified this as a 'Chromium catches up' feature rather than a new primitive.
intake.limitations: Intent thread content was not directly accessible; only the ChromeStatus record fields are known.

incubation.finding: The feature targets CSS Shapes Module Level 2 (css-shapes-2). The problem is well-defined: developers using shape-outside for float exclusion could not use rectangle shapes via rect()/xywh() and had to use workarounds (polygon, inset, orJavaScript). However, there is no evidence of developer demand research, customer interviews, or framework/ecosystem analysis in the ChromeStatus record.
incubation.limitations: No public explainer or incubation venue discussion was found beyond the spec link. No developer research or workaround cost analysis.

prototype.finding: Implementation was completed (shipped in Chrome 149), but no evidence of prototype-stage friction logs, API ergonomics testing, or framework integration spikes is available in the public record.
prototype.limitations: Prototype-stage evidence is not publicly documented.

developerTrials.finding: This is a CSS property expansion aligning with existing Firefox/Safari behavior, not a new API requiring developer trial feedback. The feature shipped directly to stable.

wideReview.finding: The spec (CSS Shapes Module Level 2) is an established CSS Working Group deliverable. Firefox and Safari already support rect() and xywh() in shape-outside, providing cross-engine validation. The feature was categorized as 'is_official_web_feature: true' with web_feature 'shape-outside'.
wideReview.limitations: Direct TAG review or Mozilla/WebKit position threads for this specific feature were not found in the public record.

experiment.finding: No origin trial was needed; the feature shipped directly.

prepareToShip.finding: Documentation and samples readiness is unknown from the public ChromeStatus record. MDN documentation state for rect()/xywh() in shape-outside was not verified.
prepareToShip.limitations: No evidence of docs readiness, FAQ, or known-limitations documentation.

release.finding: Shipped in Chrome 149 (Enabled by default, 2026-06-02). The feature aligns Chrome with Firefox and Safari, completing cross-engine interoperability for rect() and xywh() in shape-outside.

adoption.finding: The CSS property-family counter 'shape-outside' (ChromeStatus CSS timeline, bucket_id 347) measures all uses of shape-outside (not specific to rect()/xywh()). It measured 0.00652203 (~0.65%) of measured Chrome HTTP/HTTPS page loads on 2026-07-21 and has been tracked since 2013-08-30 (4412 timeline points). The family-level usage is low but stable, indicating shape-outside is a niche but persistent CSS feature. Adoption of rect()/xywh() specifically cannot be isolated from this counter. The property first appeared well before this feature shipped, so the counter does not measure the increment from rect()/xywh() support.
adoption.limitations: The counter measures the shape-outside property family, not the rect()/xywh() functions specifically. The ~0.65% usage predates and does not isolate the Chrome 149 change. No feature-specific counter exists.

support.finding: No public evidence of support issues, developer confusion, or known bugs related to rect()/xywh() in shape-outside.

deprecation.finding: This is a capability addition, not a deprecation.

developerValue.finding: Developers can now use rect() and xywh() for rectangular float exclusion shapes without workarounds. This removes a known interoperability gap where Chrome lagged Firefox and Safari.

endUserImpact.finding: End-user impact is indirect: better shape-outside support enables richer print-style and magazine layouts, but shape-outside itself is used on ~0.65% of page loads. The specific rect()/xywh() functions do not change performance, privacy, or accessibility characteristics of existing shape-outside usage.

adoption.finding: shape-outside family usage is measured at ~0.65% of Chrome page loads (CSS counter bucket_id 347, 2026-07-21). This is a stable, low-volume CSS property. The rect()/xywh() increment cannot be isolated. The feature aligns Chrome with Firefox/Safari, so adoption is primarily about parity rather than new demand.

interoperability.finding: This feature completes cross-engine support: Firefox and Safari already support rect() and xywh() in shape-outside. Chrome 149 brings parity. The web_feature 'shape-outside' is an official WebDX feature.

implementationQuality.finding: No public evidence of implementation bugs or regressions. The feature is a straightforward CSS parsing/rendering addition.

evidenceAndCommunication.finding: ChromeStatus record is clear and well-structured. No evidence of blog posts, release notes, or developer communication beyond the ChromeStatus entry. The feature has 39 stars, suggesting moderate developer awareness.

overall.finding: A well-scoped interoperability fix that closes a known CSS gap. The feature is low-risk (aligning with existing cross-engine behavior) and targets a niche but real CSS use case. The main limitation is that adoption of the specific rect()/xywh() functions cannot be measured independently of the broader shape-outside property family.

supports: ChromeStatus record with feature details, owner, category, spec link, and launch milestone
sources.limitations: ChromeStatus record fields only; intent thread content not directly accessible.

sources.publisher: Chrome Platform Status (CSS property popularity timeline)
supports: CSS property-family counter 'shape-outside' (bucket_id 347) measures all uses of shape-outside property; latest 0.00652203 (~0.65%) on 2026-07-21; tracked since 2013-08-30 with 4412 data points
sources.limitations: Family-level counter measuring the shape-outside property, not the rect()/xywh() functions specifically. Does not isolate the Chrome 149 increment. Usage predates the feature.

successes: Well-scoped interoperability fix: Chrome aligned with Firefox and Safari for rect()/xywh() in shape-outside
Low-risk CSS parsing/rendering addition with no known performance or security concerns
Clear ChromeStatus record with proper categorization and ownership
failures: No developer research or demand analysis prior to implementation
No feature-specific usage counter — adoption of rect()/xywh() cannot be measured independently
No evidence of prototype-stage friction logs or framework integration testing
Documentation readiness (MDN, samples) not verified in the public record
counterfactuals: If shape-outside usage were near zero, the interoperability gap might not justify implementation effort — but ~0.65% of page loads is non-trivial for a CSS property
If Firefox/Safari had not already shipped this, Chrome would have been the originator and would need more careful design review
A feature-specific counter for rect()/xywh() would enable better adoption measurement
skillImprovements: Add a mandatory metrics step to the retrospective flow: for every feature with any mapped Chrome use counter, cache the ChromeStatus timeline data and report the measured usage with interpretation guards
For 'Chromium catches up' features, verify and cite the specific Firefox/Safari release versions that first shipped the capability
For CSS features mapped to property-family counters, note that family-level usage does not isolate the specific function/value adoption

missingEvidence: Intent thread content (motivation, scope, requested feedback)
Prototype-stage friction logs or framework integration testing
MDN documentation state for rect()/xywh() in shape-outside
Feature-specific usage counter (only family-level shape-outside counter exists)
Direct TAG/Mozilla/WebKit position threads for this feature
completion.notes: Report created with cached Chrome usage metrics. Feature-specific adoption cannot be measured independently of the shape-outside property family counter.
